title: Re-submission of login form after failed authentication attempt without changing credentials does not cause silent failure or UI freeze
description: After a first failed login (wrong password), verifies that immediately re-clicking Submit with the exact same incorrect credentials produces a fresh, visible error response — not a spinner that never resolves, a disabled button that stays disabled, or a silent no-op. A real production pattern: the submit handler sets a 'loading' boolean that is never reset on API error, permanently disabling the button until page refresh.
expected_result: PASS: Both attempts return a visible, user-readable error; the submit button is re-enabled after each failure; no UI freeze, permanent spinner, or JavaScript exception occurs in the console. FAIL: Button remains disabled after first failure (user must refresh), error message does not reappear on second attempt, page becomes unresponsive, or a JS runtime error is logged to console.

test_steps.action: Fill in the email field with an existing account email
Fill in the password field with an incorrect password
Click the submit button to trigger first failed login attempt
test_steps.expected: A login error message appears (e.g., 'Invalid email or password')

test_steps.action: Wait for the error message to appear after the first failed attempt
test_steps.expected: Error message 'Invalid email or password' or equivalent is visible on screen

test_steps.action: Verify the submit button is still enabled and interactive after the first failure
test_steps.expected: Submit button is enabled (not disabled, not showing a permanent spinner)

test_steps.action: Click the submit button again WITHOUT changing any field values
test_steps.expected: A second login attempt is initiated; loading indicator appears briefly

test_steps.action: Wait for the error message to reappear after the second failed attempt
test_steps.expected: Error message is visible again, confirming the second request completed and error state was refreshed

test_steps.action: Verify the submit button is still enabled after second failure
test_steps.expected: Submit button remains enabled for a potential third attempt
